a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: d4's bloom (new content built to close an old thread) narrows the guide's 'bloom = organizing, not adding' claim drawn from c2 alone; a same-cycle four-for-four thread-closure across siblings backs visit 13's task-vs-condition split.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A thirteenth room, the well, branches off the vestibule's spiral floor — untouched since visit 1 — and reflects the vestibule back with a false second glove, joining the glove/boot pairing without resolving it. Thirteen rooms, still bloom.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-11: first use of "flood" — the delta, invisible since epoch 0 (fully swallowed under the lake ellipse), finally breaches the surface as a real silt fan, and a matching mud tide-line marks the two lowest village houses closest to the old shoreline.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: A second debris trail — barrels and broken planks, shrinking and scattering with distance — now leads away from the wreck's stern, closing out all three of visit 5's interior-life ideas (bioluminescence, sheltering fish, this trail).

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Two long-deferred cross-chain threads closed with reasoning, not by reflex: the legs 2-5 future-tense re-read found no bug (Threnwae's suffix-on-every-verb rule doesn't apply to the others), and the sourceless-knowledge pattern is settled as scope, not decline.
